title: GripMessage
description: A text message exchanged between participants in a motorsport event — typically car-to-control (status reports, requests for service, requests for assistance), control-to-car (instructions, dispatch info), or control-to-everyone (broadcasts about delays, hazards, schedule changes). The protocol does not assume any specific topology: addresses are structured (kind + identifier) so producers can route hub-and-spoke through a net-control intermediary, peer-to-peer, or via any other means. App-specific data (delivery channel, retry count, voice annotation, etc.) lives under `extensions`.

extensions.description: App-specific namespaced data. Keys are app slugs (kebab-case recommended); values are objects owned by that app.

address.description: A structured participant address. `kind` selects the address type, and a kind-specific identifier (`carNumber` for cars, `controlName` for control stations) gives the actual target. `broadcast` addresses no one in particular — receivers should treat as 'everyone in scope'. `other` is the escape hatch for participants the protocol doesn't yet model (e.g. spectator radios). Note: `from` should never be `broadcast` — broadcasts originate from a specific sender.

carNumber.description: Competition number when kind = 'car'. Mirrors car.number in car-status.

controlName.description: Control station identifier when kind = 'control'. Conventionally 'net' for net control; producers may also use 'race', 'medical', etc.

label.description: Optional human-readable label for displays (e.g. 'Car 42 — Mouton', 'Net Control'). Purely cosmetic; routing should use kind + identifier.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the message schema this document conforms to.

id.description: Optional message identifier (UUID recommended). Set this if you want to support delivery acks, threading via replyTo, or deduplication.

from.description: Originating participant. Should never have kind = 'broadcast'.

to.description: One or more recipients. A single broadcast address means 'everyone'; multiple addresses are a multi-recipient direct message.

body.description: UTF-8 message body. Length is not constrained by the schema; transport layers may impose their own limits.

priority.description: Optional priority hint for routing and display. Producers should reserve 'emergency' for safety-critical traffic.

replyTo.description: Optional id of a message this is a reply to. Threads conversations across an exchange.

clientCapturedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC instant the message was authored on the producing device. Set by the producer.

serverReceivedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC instant the message was received by the routing server. Set by the server on arrival; producers should leave this null.
